t: Establishing a certificate-based connection to APNs
a: Secure your communications with Apple Push Notification service (APNs) by installing a certificate on your provider server.

t: Establishing a connection to Apple Push Notification service (APNs)
a: Secure your communications with APNs to send API requests.

t: Establishing a token-based connection to APNs
a: Secure your communications with Apple Push Notification service (APNs) by using stateless authentication tokens.
Send notifications to the user’s device with a JSON payload.

t: Handling Communication Notifications and Focus Status Updates
a: Create a richer calling and messaging experience in your app by implementing communication notifications and Focus status updates.

t: Handling error responses from Apple Push Notification service
a: Respond to the status codes returned by APNs servers.

t: Modifying content in newly delivered notifications
a: Modify the payload of a remote notification before it’s displayed on the user’s iOS device.

t: Pushing background updates to your App
a: Deliver notifications that wake your app and update it in the background.

t: Scheduling a notification locally from your app
a: Create and schedule notifications from your app when you want to get the user’s attention.

t: Sending broadcast push notification requests to APNs
a: Transmit your broadcast notification payload to Apple Push Notifications service (APNs).

t: Sending channel management requests to APNs
a: Manage channels that your application uses for broadcast push notifications.
Transmit your remote notification payload and device token information to Apple Push Notification service (APNs).

t: Sending push notifications using command-line tools
a: Use basic macOS command-line tools to send push notifications to Apple Push Notification service (APNs).

t: Sending web push notifications in web apps and browsers
a: Update your web server and website to send push notifications that work in Safari, other browsers, and web apps, following cross-browser standards.

t: Setting up a remote notification server
a: Generate notifications and push them to user devices.
Enable broadcast capability for Apple Push Notifications service (APNs).

t: Testing notifications using the Push Notification Console
a: Send test notifications and access delivery logs to test your app’s integration with Apple Push Notification service (APNs).
Debug your server to send push notifications with device and broadcast push notifications.
